# eFlash FTL ˇŞ Full Project Documentation for LLM Context

## Project Identity

- Name: eFlash FTL
- Type: Embedded Flash Translation Layer library
- Language: C11 (portable, no C99 features required)
- License: MIT
- Location: eflash_ftl/eflash.h (single public header)
- Total code: ~4000 lines of C
- Total tests: 73 (100% pass)
- Key dependencies: None (zero external dependencies)

## Architecture Overview

eFlash FTL is a 4-layer architecture:

Layer 1: Public API (eflash.h) ˇŞ single header, all public functions & types
Layer 2: FTL Core (eflash_ftl.c) ˇŞ Radix Tree, GC, Transactions, Recovery, Cache, Code Region
Layer 3: Space Manager (eflash_mgr.c) ˇŞ Free list with dynamic extension, page allocation
Layer 4: ECC (ecc/bch.c, ecc/gf13.c) ˇŞ BCH 3-bit ECC with GF(2^13)

The hardware abstraction is a 5th layer: eflash_sim.c for testing, replaceable with real driver.

## Core Data Structures

### Page Layout (512 bytes per page)
- Meta area: 24 bytes (sector_id, flags, etc.)
- Epoch: 8 bytes (64-bit counter for recovery priority)
- Transaction ID: 4 bytes
- ECC: 5 bytes (BCH parity)
- User data: 464 bytes (total = 24 + 8 + 4 + 5 + 7 + 464 = 512)

### Radix Tree
- 16-level MSB-first bit comparison
- Each node: ftl_meta_t containing sector_id, epoch, global_count, txn_status, adr[16]
- Root is stored in FTL->root_page (PPN)
- Lookup: trace_tree(root, sector_id) ˇŞ O(16) constant time
- Pages are marked COMMITTED (0x21), READY (0xAD), INVALID (0x00), or BLANK (0xFF)

### GC Ring Buffer
- gc_head_page: points to next free physical page for writing
- gc_tail_page: points to next page to scan for GC
- Both wrap around: (ptr + 1) % EFLASH_TOTAL_PAGES
- is_page_still_valid(ppn) checks if page is referenced by Radix Tree
- gc_migrate_page(ppn) reads valid page, writes to new location, updates tree
- Emergency mode: gc_collect_emergency() recycles all invalid pages

### Transaction System
- Uses shadow_root: copy of current root, modified during transaction
- eflash_ftl_txn_begin(): creates shadow_root copy
- During transaction: all writes go to new pages, shadow_root updated
- eflash_ftl_txn_commit(): marks pages as COMMITTED, sets shadow_root as new root
- eflash_ftl_txn_commit_with_update(): word-level update (1ˇú0 transitions only)
- eflash_ftl_txn_abort(): discards shadow_root, marks pages as INVALID

### Write-Back Cache
- 4-slot LRU cache (g_page_cache[PAGE_CACHE_SLOTS])
- Each slot: valid, dirty, sector_id, data[USER_DATA_SIZE], cache_seq
- Write-back: data goes to cache first, evicted to flash when full or on commit
- Write-through: data goes directly to flash (used as fallback)
- FLUSH_THRESHOLD = 2: flush when 2 dirty pages
- content_cache_flush_all() called on txn_commit()

### Free List (Space Manager)
- Base: 4 pages, 228 free nodes
- Extension: 4 pages per level, 116 nodes per level, max 4 levels
- free_node_t: next_free (PPN), count (pages in this block)
- Allocation: best-fit algorithm
- Merging: adjacent free blocks merged on release

## Complete Public API

### Lifecycle
```c
eflash_ftl_t* eflash_get_ftl(void);  // Returns global static instance
int eflash_ftl_init(void);           // Initialize FTL (may recover from power loss)
void eflash_ftl_deinit(void);       // Cleanup

// Flash simulation (replace with hardware)
int eflash_init(const char *filename);
void eflash_deinit(void);
int eflash_hw_erase(uint16_t page_addr);
int eflash_hw_prog(uint16_t page_addr, const uint8_t *data);
int eflash_hw_read(uint16_t page_addr, uint8_t *data);
int eflash_hw_word_update(uint16_t page_addr, uint16_t offset, uint16_t data);
int eflash_hw_is_blank(uint16_t page_addr);
```

### Core I/O
```c
int eflash_ftl_read(uint16_t sector_id, uint8_t *data);
int eflash_ftl_write(uint16_t sector_id, const uint8_t *data);
int eflash_ftl_write_back(uint16_t sector_id, const uint8_t *data);
int eflash_ftl_write_through(uint16_t sector_id, const uint8_t *data);
```

### Transaction
```c
int eflash_ftl_txn_begin(void);
int eflash_ftl_txn_commit(void);
int eflash_ftl_txn_commit_with_update(uint16_t sector, uint16_t offset, uint16_t new_word);
int eflash_ftl_txn_abort(void);
```

### Garbage Collection
```c
int eflash_ftl_gc_collect_once(void);
int eflash_ftl_gc_collect_full(void);
int eflash_ftl_gc_collect_emergency(void);
int eflash_ftl_gc_get_available_pages(void);
```

### Code Region
```c
int eflash_ftl_code_migrate_from_logical(uint16_t src_lpn, uint16_t num_pages);
int eflash_ftl_code_expand(uint16_t additional_pages);
int eflash_ftl_code_shrink(uint16_t pages_to_free);
int eflash_ftl_code_delete_segment(uint16_t code_lpn, uint16_t num_pages);
int eflash_ftl_code_read(uint16_t code_lpn, uint8_t *data);
int eflash_ftl_code_recover(void);
int eflash_ftl_code_get_state(void);
```

### Object Headers
```c
int eflash_ftl_obj_set_header(uint16_t obj_id, const obj_header_t *hdr);
int eflash_ftl_obj_get_header(uint16_t obj_id, obj_header_t *hdr);
int eflash_ftl_obj_free_header(uint16_t obj_id);
```

## Key Constants

- EFLASH_PAGE_SIZE: 512
- EFLASH_TOTAL_PAGES: 2048 (1MB total)
- USER_DATA_SIZE: 464 (per page)
- RADIX_DEPTH: 16
- META_SIZE: 48
- PAGE_CACHE_SLOTS: 4
- FLUSH_THRESHOLD: 2
- MAX_EXT_LEVELS: 16 (object headers)
- MAX_MGR_EXT_LEVELS: 4 (free list)
- TXN_STATUS_COMMITTED: 0x21
- TXN_STATUS_READY: 0xAD
- TXN_STATUS_INVALID: 0x00
- TXN_STATUS_BLANK: 0xFF
- PAGE_NONE: 0xFFFF

## Power-Loss Recovery

Recovery process (eflash_ftl_init ˇú find_root_full_scan):

1. Scan all 2048 physical pages
2. For each page, read metadata (ftl_meta_t)
3. Check: txn_status == COMMITTED (0x21)
4. Track: highest epoch value
5. Track: highest global_count among pages with max epoch
6. The page with max(epoch, global_count) is the root page
7. Recover free list: scan all pages, find free nodes, rebuild free page list
8. Recover GC pointers: head = first free page, tail = head
9. Recover code region: check migration state, resume if IN_PROGRESS
10. Rebuild cache: all cache slots invalidated, will be populated on next write

Recovery guarantees:
- Uncommitted transactions: pages are INVALID, will be GC'd
- Committed transactions: fully preserved
- Code region partial migration: resumed from checkpoint
- ECC errors during scan: page is skipped, not used as root

## Test Coverage

### Basic Tests (26 tests)
- test_init: FTL initialization
- test_read_write: basic read/write
- test_multiple_writes: multiple writes to same sector
- test_gc_basic: basic garbage collection
- test_gc_emergency: emergency GC mode
- test_txn_basic: basic transaction
- test_txn_abort: transaction abort
- test_txn_commit_with_update: word update optimization
- test_ecc_basic: ECC detection/correction
- test_ecc_multibit: multi-bit error correction
- test_power_loss_consistency: power loss recovery
- test_power_loss_partial_cache: partial cache recovery
- test_write_back_cache: write-back cache
- test_write_back_cache_stress: cache stress test
- test_write_back_cache_exhausted: cache exhaustion fallback
- test_mgr_alloc_free: space manager allocation
- test_mgr_extension: space manager extension
- test_obj_header: object header management
- test_obj_header_extension: object header extension
- test_sector_range: sector range operations
- test_page_overflow: page overflow protection
- test_radix_tree: Radix tree operations
- test_concurrent_access: concurrent read/write
- test_init_recovery: initialization with recovery
- test_cache_flush: cache flush on commit
- test_max_pages: maximum page allocation

### Code Region Tests (19 tests)
- test_code_migrate_basic: basic code migration
- test_code_migrate_large: large code migration
- test_code_migrate_power_failure: migration with power failure
- test_code_expand: code region expansion
- test_code_shrink: code region shrinking
- test_code_delete: code segment deletion
- test_code_read: code region read
- test_code_gc: code region GC coexistence
- test_code_data_coexistence: code and data coexistence
- test_code_recovery: code region recovery
- test_code_boundary: code region boundary conditions
- test_code_multi_migrate: multiple migrations
- test_code_full_region: full region migration
- test_code_expand_shrink_cycle: expand/shrink cycle
- test_code_delete_partial: partial segment deletion
- test_code_migrate_zero: zero-page migration
- test_code_max_expand: maximum expansion
- test_code_shrink_all: shrink to zero
- test_code_recovery_in_progress: recovery of in-progress migration

### Extension Tests (27 tests)
- test_power_loss_single: single power loss
- test_power_loss_multi: multiple power loss
- test_power_loss_during_gc: power loss during GC
- test_extreme_power_loss: extreme power loss
- test_max_capacity: maximum capacity
- test_ecc_boundary: ECC boundary conditions
- test_gc_stress: GC stress test
- test_txn_stress: transaction stress test
- test_free_list_max: free list maximum
- test_obj_header_max: object header maximum
- test_radix_edge: Radix tree edge cases
- test_cache_edge: cache edge cases
- test_space_exhaustion: space exhaustion
- test_large_write: large write operations
- test_repeated_write: repeated writes
- test_sector_boundary: sector boundary conditions
- test_gc_full_cycle: full GC cycle
- test_txn_rollback: transaction rollback
- test_ecc_detect_4bit: ECC 4-bit error detection
- test_power_loss_code_region: power loss during code region operations
- test_concurrent_gc_write: concurrent GC and write
- test_max_extension_levels: maximum extension levels
- test_free_list_fragmentation: free list fragmentation
- test_obj_header_fragmentation: object header fragmentation
- test_code_region_fragmentation: code region fragmentation
- test_cache_thrashing: cache thrashing
- test_long_running: long-running operations

### Stability Test (1 test)
- test_long_term_stability: 100K random ops + 1000 mixed-size writes + 10 power cycles + final 100-sector verification

## Known Bugs Fixed

### P1: GC head==tail data loss (eflash_ftl.c:2593)
When gc_head_page == gc_tail_page and the page was still valid, the original code moved the head pointer without migrating the data. Fixed by adding gc_migrate_page() call before pointer movement.

### P2: write_back ignored write_through error (eflash_ftl.c:1835)
When cache slots were all invalid (slot < 0), write_back called write_through but ignored its return value, always returning 0. Fixed by returning the write_through result directly.

## Porting Guide

To use on real hardware, replace these 4 functions:
1. eflash_hw_erase(page_addr) ˇŞ erase a physical page to 0xFF
2. eflash_hw_prog(page_addr, data) ˇŞ program 512 bytes to a page
3. eflash_hw_read(page_addr, data) ˇŞ read 512 bytes from a page
4. eflash_hw_word_update(page_addr, offset, data) ˇŞ program 16-bit word (1ˇú0 transitions only)

The library assumes:
- Pages are 512 bytes
- Erase sets all bits to 1
- Program can only change 1ˇú0 (NAND flash constraint)
- Word update can modify specific 16-bit words within a page (for NAND partial programming)

## Build Commands

```bash
# Basic tests
gcc -std=c11 -DFTL_DEBUG_ENABLE=0 -DEFLASH_TEST_MODE -Ieflash_ftl -Iecc \
    eflash_ftl/eflash_ftl.c eflash_ftl/eflash_ftl_tests.c eflash_ftl/eflash_mgr.c \
    eflash_ftl/eflash_sim.c ecc/bch.c ecc/gf13.c -o eflash_ftl_tests.exe

# Code region tests
gcc -std=c11 -DFTL_DEBUG_ENABLE=0 -DEFLASH_TEST_MODE -Ieflash_ftl -Iecc \
    eflash_ftl/eflash_ftl.c eflash_ftl/eflash_ftl_tests_code_region.c \
    eflash_ftl/eflash_mgr.c eflash_ftl/eflash_sim.c ecc/bch.c ecc/gf13.c \
    -o eflash_ftl_tests_code_region.exe

# Extension tests
gcc -std=c11 -DFTL_DEBUG_ENABLE=0 -DEFLASH_TEST_MODE -Ieflash_ftl -Iecc \
    eflash_ftl/eflash_ftl.c eflash_ftl/eflash_ftl_tests_extension.c \
    eflash_ftl/eflash_mgr.c eflash_ftl/eflash_sim.c eflash_ftl/eflash_ftl_visual.c \
    ecc/bch.c ecc/gf13.c -o eflash_ftl_tests_extension.exe

# Stability test
gcc -std=c11 -DFTL_DEBUG_ENABLE=0 -DEFLASH_TEST_MODE -Ieflash_ftl -Iecc \
    eflash_ftl/eflash_ftl.c eflash_ftl/eflash_ftl_tests_stability.c \
    eflash_ftl/eflash_mgr.c eflash_ftl/eflash_sim.c eflash_ftl/eflash_ftl_visual.c \
    ecc/bch.c ecc/gf13.c -o eflash_ftl_tests_stability.exe
```

## Version History

- v2.2.4 (2026-06-03): Documentation updates, test reports
- v2.2.3 (2026-06-03): DESIGN_OVERVIEW.md v2.0, REPO_WIKI updates
- v2.2.2 (2026-06-03): Makefile improvements, architecture docs
- v2.2.1 (2026-06-02): Stability fixes, test reorganization
- v2.1.0: Code region support, write-back cache, ECC integration
- v1.8.0: Initial public release with basic FTL functionality
